findings.detail: Cycle 2: four parallel pillar cells render side-by-side as a clean categorical row under the 'The Four Pillars of AI Ethics' banner title. Matches the artifact_plan intent (four-cell comparison, not a pipeline). No overlaps, no truncation, all bold names + 12px subtitle/violation/check lines legible at 1400px viewer scale, whitespace balanced. Cycle 1 showed the four disconnected nodes stacked vertically (layout_wrong_for_intent + whitespace_imbalance); fixed by wrapping them in an LR subgraph joined with invisible ~~~ links to force a single horizontal rank.

preflight.notes: Bold node labels are the four pillar names (Fairness, Transparency, Accountability, Harm Prevention) — each traces to a §5 Core Concepts heading. Definitions, violation examples, and checklist signals sit in subtitle spans (not counted as label tokens). No bare acronyms; XAI/EU/NIST/red-teaming deliberately excluded as out-of-headline scope. Governing concept lives in title: banner, not a dangling ghost node.

stats.layout: flowchart LR, four cells in one LR subgraph on a single rank (categorical 4-cell)
